what_is_interesting: A 2,000-year-old Roman emperor's private journal — written to conquer the mind, not the world — has become one of the most powerful modern sleep aids, because true Stoic stillness is indistinguishable from surrender to sleep.
likely_image: A marble bust or dramatic portrait of Marcus Aurelius against a dark, starry, or candlelit background suggesting night and calm.
winner_reasoning: When a sleep-deprived viewer sees Marcus Aurelius's stoic face beside 'FINALLY QUIET,' it lands as a direct promise of relief — this ancient emperor will silence the racing mind that's been keeping you awake, and that emotional payoff makes clicking feel urgent.
